title: Iron Snake Level Transition Animation (Board Morph)
content:
## Overview

When a player completes a level in Iron Snake mode, the board does not swap instantly. Instead, the playable region **morphs cell-by-cell** from the old shape into the new one, driven by a `requestAnimationFrame` loop paced to the snake's own move interval. The snake never moves or teleports during the transition; the new board is grown around the snake's current cells.

## Architecture

### The key lever: a single mask field

All three board-drawing functions (`drawGroundPlane`, `drawGridLines`, `drawGroundBorder`) read one field — `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` (flat `gridSize × gridSize`, `1` = playable). Temporarily assigning an **interpolated display mask** to `this.boardMask` and calling the existing `draw()` causes the floor tiles, grid lines, and border outline to all reshape for free, with no changes to any rendering code.

### Board generation: `growIronSnakeBoard` (`src/iron-snake.ts`)

The new board is not generated independently and then fitted to the snake. Instead, `growIronSnakeBoard` seeds a clear room around the snakes' combined bounding box and grows fresh overlapping rooms outward until the board reaches target fill. This guarantees:

- Every snake cell is playable and connected by construction — no relocation needed.
- The surrounding region is always regenerated fresh, so the shape changes every level regardless of whether the grid size increases.

The previous approach embedded the old board into the new grid and added rooms until target fill. When the grid size stayed the same (common at early levels floored at size 12), the old board was already at target fill and zero new rooms were added, producing an identical shape.

### Level advance: `applyLevelAdvance` (`src/main.ts`)

`applyLevelAdvance` replaces the old `reseatKeepingShape` pipeline. It:

1. Keeps both snakes exactly in place (grid coordinates preserved across the level boundary).
2. Calls `growIronSnakeBoard` with the current snake cells.
3. When the grid size grows between levels, calls `translateSnakes` to shift both snakes by the centering offset so they remain visually anchored on screen.

The entire shape-rotation reseat chain (`placeRigid`, `layoutBody`, `placeSnakeKeepingShape`, `rotateOffset`, `rotateDirection`, `reseatKeepingShape`) has been deleted as dead code. `vectorToDirection` was retained as it is still used by tail-direction rendering logic.

### Morph animation: `startLevelMorph` / `morphStep` / `finishLevelMorph` / `cancelLevelMorph` (`src/main.ts`)

**Mask construction:**

- `startDisplay` — the old board's playable cells stamped into the new grid's dimensions, center-aligned, with every current snake segment and food cell unioned in so nothing floats over void.
- `targetDisplay` — copy of the new `boardMask` produced by `growIronSnakeBoard`.
- `changed` — list of `(x, y)` positions where `startDisplay ≠ targetDisplay`, **sorted by distance from board center ascending** (inside-out wave). Cells grow outward and old-only cells peel away as the wave passes, reading as a coherent spatial event.

**Pacing:**

Each morph step is held for approximately one snake-move duration (not a fixed wall-clock tween), so the reshape unfolds at the game's own rhythm and remains perceptible at any game speed. Tuning constants: `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEPS`, `LEVEL_MORPH_MAX_STEPS`, `LEVEL_MORPH_CELLS_PER_STEP`, `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEP_MS`.

**Loop lifecycle:**

- The logic `setInterval` is torn down at the start of the morph.
- The RAF loop drives `draw()` each step with the mutated working mask assigned to `this.boardMask`.
- `finishLevelMorph` restores the real `boardMask`, calls `draw()` once cleanly, and restarts the logic interval at the new `gameSpeed`.
- Guards in `updateSinglePlayer` and `updateTwoPlayer` suppress the normal per-tick repaint while `isTransitioning` is true, preventing a stray full-new-board frame from painting before the morph starts.

**Cleanup:**

`cancelLevelMorph` is called from `startNewGame` and both back-to-menu handlers to cancel any in-flight RAF and clear `isTransitioning`, preventing a morph from painting over a fresh game.

### Instance fields and constants added to `src/main.ts`

| Name | Kind | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `isTransitioning` | field (`boolean`) | Guards repaint suppression and re-entrancy |
| `transitionRafId` | field (`number \| null`) | Tracks in-flight RAF for cancellation |
| `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEPS` | constant | Minimum morph step count |
| `LEVEL_MORPH_MAX_STEPS` | constant | Maximum morph step count |
| `LEVEL_MORPH_CELLS_PER_STEP` | constant | Cells revealed per step |
| `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEP_MS` | constant | Minimum ms held per step |

## Behavior

- The snake never teleports or relocates during a level transition.
- The board shape always visibly changes between levels, even when the grid size does not increase.
- The morph always plays — the `prefers-reduced-motion` instant-swap gate that was added in an earlier iteration has been removed, because it caused the transition to be invisible to users with that OS setting enabled.
- Classic mode's `expandGrid()` is unaffected and retains its instant swap.
- If a morph is in flight when a new game starts or the player returns to the menu, `cancelLevelMorph` cleans it up immediately.

## Files

- `src/iron-snake.ts` — `growIronSnakeBoard`
- `src/main.ts` — `applyLevelAdvance`, `startLevelMorph`, `morphStep`, `finishLevelMorph`, `cancelLevelMorph`, morph fields and constants, repaint guards
